first_sentence: TraceWarden SIFT proves seventeen D F I R claims in ninety seconds. Every finding needs a receipt before it survives.

scenes.title: Prove 17 DFIR claims in 90 seconds.
scenes.label: TraceWarden SIFT
scenes.caption: Every finding needs a receipt before it survives.
scenes.narration: TraceWarden SIFT proves seventeen D F I R claims in ninety seconds. Every finding needs a receipt before it survives.

scenes.title: A false lead creates a second incident.
scenes.label: Why this matters
scenes.caption: A plausible claim must earn support.
scenes.narration: A defender agent that cannot prove its claims burns responder time. The demo starts with a plausible updater lead, then forces that claim to earn support.

scenes.title: One terminal command runs the case.
scenes.label: Live terminal execution
scenes.caption: Seal evidence, call typed tools, evaluate 17 claims.
scenes.narration: Here is the live terminal path. The agent seals the evidence root, calls typed read only parsers, evaluates seventeen claims, and writes the reports.

scenes.title: CLAIM-004 is revoked.
scenes.label: Self-correction sequence
scenes.caption: CLAIM-004 loses support and gets revoked.
scenes.narration: The updater process looked suspicious at first. The verifier found a signed vendor path and no second source, so TraceWarden revoked CLAIM zero zero four and rerouted.

scenes.title: Surviving claims point to evidence.
scenes.label: Audit trail
scenes.caption: Receipts bind findings to files, lines, hashes, and tool calls.
scenes.narration: The replacement path is receipt backed. CLAIM zero zero nine ties PowerShell to network evidence, with file names, line numbers, parser names, hashes, and tool calls.

scenes.title: The run is scored against ground truth.
scenes.label: Accuracy report
scenes.caption: 16 confirmed, 1 revoked, 0 hallucinations.
scenes.narration: The package measures truth instead of trusting the agent. Sixteen claims are confirmed, one is revoked, precision and recall are one point zero, and hallucinations are zero.

scenes.title: The guardrail sits below the planner.
scenes.label: Architecture
scenes.caption: The planner can change. The verifier contract stays deterministic.
scenes.narration: The planner can be an L L M, but the safety boundary is deterministic. Evidence is sealed, tools are read only, the verifier owns claim state, and receipts are replayable.

scenes.title: Run it, inspect it, extend it.
scenes.label: Submission package
scenes.caption: Run the command, open the receipts, extend the parser set.
scenes.narration: Everything in the video is reproducible from the repo. Run the command, open the receipts, and add the next SIFT parser family to the same verifier contract.
